skill.name: Azure Fabric Management SDK for Python
skill.description: A skill that provides prompts and guidance for working with Microsoft Azure Fabric Management SDK in Python. Helps users generate code for managing Azure Fabric resources including workspaces, notebooks, lakehouses, and data pipelines.
skill.summary: Generate Azure Fabric Management SDK code for Python to manage workspaces, notebooks, lakehouses, and data pipelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only SKILL.md with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 security issues. Risk score is 0/100. The skill provides prompts for generating Azure Fabric SDK code - there are no scripts, network calls, filesystem access, environment variable reads, or external command executions. This is a safe, informational skill for AI-assisted code generation.

content.value_statement: Writing Azure Fabric Management SDK code manually is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides AI-powered prompts that generate clean, production-ready Python code for managing Azure Fabric resources including workspaces, notebooks, lakehouses, and data pipelines.
actual_capabilities: Generate Python code for Azure Fabric workspace creation and management
Create and manage Azure Fabric notebooks programmatically
Set up and configure Azure Fabric lakehouses
Build data pipelines using Azure Fabric SDK
Handle authentication and connection to Azure Fabric
List, retrieve, update, and delete Azure Fabric resources
limitations: Does not execute code - only generates prompts for AI to produce code
Requires valid Azure subscription and credentials to use the generated code
Does not handle Azure resource provisioning costs or billing
Does not provide real-time monitoring of Azure Fabric resources

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Managing Azure Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Automate creation and management of Azure Fabric workspaces across multiple environments (dev, staging, production) using Python scripts.

use_cases.title: Data Engineer Building Data Pipelines
use_cases.description: Generate Python code to create and orchestrate data pipelines in Azure Fabric for ETL and data transformation workflows.

use_cases.title: Developer Building Cloud Applications
use_cases.description: Quickly generate Python code snippets for integrating Azure Fabric resources into custom applications without studying the full SDK documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a New Fabric Workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Python code using Azure Fabric Management SDK to create a new Fabric workspace with the name '{workspace_name}' in resource group '{resource_group}'. Include proper error handling and authentication setup.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a new workspace in Azure Fabric

prompt_templates.title: List All Workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code to list all Azure Fabric workspaces in a subscription using the management SDK. Include pagination handling and output formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Lakehouse
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Python code to create an Azure Fabric lakehouse named '{lakehouse_name}' in workspace '{workspace_name}'. Include the OneLake short paths configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Data Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Python code to create an Azure Fabric data pipeline with the following specifications: name '{pipeline_name}', description '{description}', and activities for data ingestion from '{source_path}' to '{destination_path}'.

output: Step 1: Set up authentication using DefaultAzureCredential
Step 2: Create a FabricManagementClient with your subscription
Step 3: Define workspace properties (name, location, capacity)
Step 5: Handle the long-running operation and check provisioning state
This example shows how to create a lakehouse:
2. Initialize FabricApiClient with your workspace endpoint
3. Define lakehouse payload with name and description
5. The response includes lakehouse ID and OneLake paths

best_practices: Always use managed identity or service principal for authentication in production environments instead of storing credentials in code
Implement proper error handling with try-except blocks and specific exception types from the Azure SDK
Use async versions of SDK methods (if available) for better performance in high-throughput applications
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode Azure credentials or connection strings in your source code - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Avoid making synchronous calls in tight loops - implement batching or async patterns for bulk operations
Do not ignore API response statuses - always check for success and handle failure cases properly

faq.question: What is Azure Fabric?
faq.answer: Azure Fabric (now Microsoft Fabric) is an analytics platform that unifies data engineering, data warehousing, real-time analytics, and business intelligence into a single product.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates prompts that help create Python code. You will need an Azure subscription to actually run the generated code.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: The generated code supports Azure DefaultAzureCredential, service principal, managed identity, and Azure CLI authentication.

faq.question: Can this skill manage all Azure Fabric resources?
faq.answer: This skill covers workspaces, notebooks, lakehouses, and pipelines. Some advanced features may require additional prompt customization.

faq.question: Is the generated code production-ready?
faq.answer: The generated code includes error handling and follows Azure SDK best practices, but you should review and test it before deploying to production.

faq.question: Which Python version is required?
faq.answer: The Azure Fabric Management SDK requires Python 3.8 or later. Check the SDK documentation for specific version requirements.
